title: Taiwan Strait Blockade
subtitle: Real-time monitoring and analysis of the Taiwan Strait security environment. Covering military posturing, diplomatic developments, and economic impacts.
core_assessment: The Taiwan Strait is the single highest-consequence fracture point in the global system. A successful Chinese blockade or kinetic action against Taiwan would generate an estimated $10 trillion in first-year global economic damage, collapse the semiconductor supply chain that underpins every advanced economy, and force the most consequential US strategic decision since 1962. The question is not whether this fracture is possible — it is whether the international community has built the decision architecture to manage it before it becomes irreversible.

key_actors.name: China
key_actors.stake: PLA Navy amphibious assault capability; economic coercion toolkit.

key_actors.name: United States
key_actors.stake: Forward-deployed forces; semiconductor supply chain dependency.

key_actors.name: Japan
key_actors.stake: Defensive modernization (ASCM, mobile air defence); domestic resilience.

key_actors.name: Taiwan
key_actors.stake: US alliance obligations; geographic exposure to blockade.
